text.title: rust-testing-property Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for rust-testing-property. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Any Rust crate containing parsers, codecs, serialization, or pure mathematical functions MUST have at least one proptest! block covering the primary invariant (e.g., decode(encode(x)) == x, sorted output is monotone, result is in [0, 1]).
rule.rationale: Hand-written tables cover the happy path and the author's mental model of edge cases. proptest covers the cases the author did not think of, which are exactly the cases that produce production bugs.

rule.statement: Record and commit failing proptest seeds. When a CI run fails, extract the PROPTEST_SEED from the output and add it as a regression test: proptest::test_runner::Config { failure_persistence: Some(Box::new(FileFailurePersistence::WithSource("regression"))) }. Do not discard seeds.
rule.rationale: A seed that reproduced a bug is a regression test for free. Discarding it means the same bug can reappear undetected.

rule.statement: Never use assert_eq! to compare f64 values in tests. Use approx::assert_relative_eq!(a, b, epsilon = 1e-9) or approx::assert_abs_diff_eq!. This applies to both example-based and property-based tests.
rule.rationale: assert_eq! on f64 fails when floating-point arithmetic produces representation differences (e.g., 0.1 + 0.2 != 0.3). approx comparisons express the actual tolerance requirement.
